hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance SSTR2-specific ECL2/TM5 interactions, while mutating position 6 to acidic residues (E/D) will disrupt off-target (SSTR3/5) electrostatic complementarity, thereby increasing selectivity margin.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current design strategy is generating high-affinity ligands but not sufficiently disrupting off-target interactions. A focused mutation strategy at non-pharmacophore positions is necessary to achieve SSTR2 selectivity.

proposed_changes.rationale: To improve selectivity, we must disrupt off-target contacts at non-pharmacophore positions (1/2/5/6/11/12) that are conserved in SSTR3/SSTR5 but not in SSTR2.
These mutations are designed to break conserved interactions in SSTR3/SSTR5 while maintaining flexibility and avoiding disruption of the pharmacophore.
